description: The fingernail: the small hard shield at the finger's ('thumai') end. The last piece of the hand.
sound_symbolism: 'ti' the tip, 'pi' the smallness, 'ka' the hardness — the object built syllable by syllable.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'mia lo tipika kati.' (I cut my fingernails.) 'lopia tipika thiku nai.' (The child's fingernails are small.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The nail grows without being asked — the body's quietest demonstration of impermanence and renewal, trimmed away and returning.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Grooming was the original hygiene and the original tenderness — nails trimmed by a parent's careful hand.
